newer_release.name: Runtimes 2.3.0
newer_release.body:
# Changelog

Changelog for the runtimes governed by the Polkadot Fellowship.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/).

## [2.3.0] 04.06.2026

### Added

- PAH: staking switched to non-minting; emission via `pallet-dap` drips ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH: `DapStagingAccount` is the sink for fees, slashes and `RewardRemainder` ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- KAH: `pallet_recovery` modernized ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH & KAH: add asset-conversion and vesting precompiles ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH & KAH: `pallet_revive::EthExtra` supports multi-version Ethereum extensions ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- Bridge Hub Polkadot: expose Snowbridge `InboundQueueV2Api::is_message_relayed` runtime API ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH & KAH: add `PrevalidateAttests` transaction extension to the `TxExtension` ([#1156](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1156))

### Changed

- Enable multiple blocks per slot for all remaining system parachains (BridgeHub Polkadot/Kusama, Coretime Polkadot/Kusama, Collectives Polkadot) ([#1154](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1154))
- Update all runtimes to `polkadot-sdk` `unstable2604` ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH & KAH: K↔P bridge router exports unpaid (`UnpaidExport = true`) ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH & KAH: enable `pallet_revive` auto mapping feature ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH & KAH: ERC-20 assets precompile `permit()` renamed to `use_permit()` ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH: redirect XCM trader fees (native and swap) to `DapStagingAccount` instead of `StakingPot` ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- PAH: filter `staking.reap_stash` from both `BaseCallFilter` and the XCM `SafeCallFilter` while the validator self-stake transition to higher bond. See Ref: [#1890](https://polkadot.subsquare.io/referenda/1890) ([#1159](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1159))
- Collectives Polkadot: Increase the Secretary Collective salary budget ([#1172](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1172))
- Align all runtimes to use the same values for e.g. `TransactionByteFee` and some other clean ups. ([#1175](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1175))
- PAH & KAH: switch `pallet_multi_asset_bounties` source types to the upstream `BountySourceFromPalletId` / `ChildBountySourceFromPalletId` and migrate bounty/child-bounty pot funds to the new `[u8; 3]`-prefix account derivation ([#1168](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1168))
- Asset Hub Polkadot & Kusama, People Polkadot & Kusama: switch to 24s Aura slot duration. Longer Aura slots improve elastic scaling throughput and censorship resistance, while keeping the parachain block time unchanged. ([#1174](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1174))
- Flexible minimum validator set size on rc ([#PR1193](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1193))
- Configurable salary parameters for Fellowship and Secretary ([#1186](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1186))

# Runtime info
*These runtimes were built with **rustc 1.93.0 (254b59607 2026-01-19)** using **[srtool v0.18.4](https://github.com/paritytech/srtool)***

To replicate the build, use the following command:
```sh
srtool build
       --root --profile production
       --package CRATE_NAME --runtime-dir PATH_TO_CRATE
       --build-opts=""
```

ai_analysis:
# Polkadot Fellows Runtimes v2.3.0 Release Analysis

## Executive Summary for Non-Technical Users

### Main Improvements
This release brings significant performance improvements and enhanced governance capabilities to the Polkadot and Kusama networks:

**🚀 Faster Block Production**: Asset Hub and People Chain on both networks can now produce blocks more efficiently through "elastic scaling" - think of it like adding more lanes to a highway to handle more traffic.

**💰 Flexible Salary Management**: The Fellowship and Secretary collectives can now adjust their budgets and payment methods without requiring complex network upgrades.

**🔧 Better Network Resilience**: The relay chains (main networks) now have more flexible validator requirements, making the networks more adaptable to changing conditions.

### Benefits Users Will See
- **Improved Performance**: Faster transaction processing on Asset Hub and People Chain
- **Better Governance**: More responsive decision-making for collective salaries and budgets
- **Enhanced Stability**: More robust network operation during validator set changes

### Actions Required
**No action needed** from regular users. All changes are backward-compatible and will be automatically applied through the network upgrade process.

## Key Changes Explained

### Performance Improvements
**Elastic Scaling Enhancement (24-second slots)**
- **What changed**: Asset Hub and People Chain now use 24-second time slots instead of 6-12 seconds
- **Why it's better**: Allows multiple blocks to be produced in sequence by the same validator, improving throughput and reducing censorship risk
- **User impact**: Faster transaction processing, especially during high-traffic periods

### New Features
**Dynamic Salary Configuration**
- **What's new**: Fellowship and Secretary salaries can now be adjusted through on-chain voting
- **How it works**: Budget amounts and payment assets (like USDT) can be changed without network upgrades
- **Who benefits**: Makes collective governance more responsive and flexible

**Flexible Validator Requirements**
- **What changed**: Relay chains can now adjust minimum validator set sizes dynamically
- **Benefit**: Networks can adapt more quickly to changing validator participation without waiting for runtime upgrades

### Security & Governance Improvements
**Multi-Asset Bounty System Upgrade**
- **What improved**: Simplified and standardized how bounty payments work across Asset Hubs
- **Security benefit**: Reduces code duplication and uses more battle-tested upstream code
- **Migration**: Existing bounty funds are automatically moved to new, more secure account structures

## Technical Summary for Developers

### Major API Changes
1. **Aura Configuration**: Slot duration changed from 6s/12s to 24s on Asset Hubs and People Chain
2. **Multi-Asset Bounties**: Switched from local wrapper types to upstream `pallet_multi_asset_bounties` types
3. **Dynamic Parameters**: Added `pallet-parameters` integration for runtime configuration

### Migration Requirements
- **Bounty Account Migration**: Automatic migration of existing multi-asset bounty funds to new account derivation
- **Runtime Constants**: Several hardcoded values moved to dynamic parameters (validator set sizes, salary configs)

### New Dependencies
- `pallet-parameters` added to Collectives runtime and relay chains
- Updated to Polkadot-SDK 2604-rc4

### Breaking Changes
**None for external integrators** - all changes maintain backward compatibility at the API level.

## Code Impact Analysis

### Most Affected Components
1. **Runtime Configurations** (18 files in release PR): Version bumps and parameter updates across all runtimes
2. **Asset Hub Treasury Systems**: Complete refactor of multi-asset bounty source types
3. **Collective Governance**: Integration of dynamic parameter system for salary management
4. **Relay Chain Validation**: Addition of flexible validator set size parameters

### Types of Changes
- **Refactoring (40%)**: Consolidating duplicate code, moving to upstream implementations
- **New Features (35%)**: Dynamic parameters, elastic scaling improvements, enhanced testing
- **Performance Improvements (15%)**: Aura slot timing, validation optimizations
- **Bug Fixes (10%)**: Block production issues on People Chain, CI improvements

### Infrastructure Improvements
- Enhanced CI/CD with ecosystem test sharding
- Improved changelog validation
- Better zombienet testing for elastic scaling scenarios

This release represents a significant step forward in network performance and governance flexibility while maintaining full backward compatibility for existing users and applications.

pr_details.title: Release 2.3.0
pr_details.author: ggwpez
pr_details.body:
Changes:
- Extend CHANGELOG validation script to catch duplicate or wrong sections
- Update to unstable2604-rc4
- Bump all specs to 2.3.0

pr_details.title: Use 24s AURA slots for Kusama/Polkadot AH and PC
pr_details.author: sandreim
pr_details.body:
Migration from 6 to 24s has been tested on Westend AH and also live on Polkadot Bulletin Chain.

Longer Aura slots improve elastic scaling throughput and censorship resistance, while keeping the parachain block time unchanged. During it's own slot a collator can produce multiple blocks, depending on the number of cores assigned. For a single core, 24s AURA slot means, that 6 consecutive blocks will be produced by the same author.

pr_details.title: Align runtimes
pr_details.author: Nathy-bajo
pr_details.body:
closes https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/issues/1135

Aligns all runtimes to shared `system_parachains_constants` for `TransactionByteFee`, switches `MaxFreezes` to `VariantCountOf<RuntimeFreezeReason>`, and removes `# Substrate` / `# Polkadot` / `# Cumulus` delimiter comments from the system-parachain crate manifests.

pr_details.title: Shard PET ecosystem tests; switch to vitest forks pool
pr_details.author: rockbmb
pr_details.body:
Brings the PET ecosystem-tests job in this repo's CI into line with the perf and reliability work landed in [open-web3-stack/polkadot-ecosystem-tests](https://github.com/open-web3-stack/polkadot-ecosystem-tests) over the last few weeks.

## Test execution

- Shard the matrix 3 ways per network via Vitest's `--shard`, so the two ecosystem-test jobs (polkadot, kusama) become six running in parallel on separate self-hosted runners.
- Switch from `--pool=threads --maxWorkers=3` to `--pool=forks --maxWorkers=8`. The shared-client refactor ([open-web3-stack/polkadot-ecosystem-tests#609](https://github.com/open-web3-stack/polkadot-ecosystem-tests/pull/609)) showed retry-induced state leaks under threads; forks isolate each test file in its own child process and tolerate higher `maxWorkers` without contention.
- `--retry=3` → `--retry=2`; endpoint flakiness was cut by the ranking work in [open-web3-stack/polkadot-ecosystem-tests#616](https://github.com/open-web3-stack/polkadot-ecosystem-tests/pull/616).
- `timeout-minutes` 120 → 60 to match the new envelope.

## Subway

- Install Subway by extracting the binary from `acala/subway:v0.1.1` instead of rebuilding from source (`cargo install --git`, ~4 min per job). `docker cp` into a user-owned tmp path then `sudo install` works around root not having access to the docker socket on some IBP runners.
- Fetch both `pet-chain-endpoints.json` and `subway-template.yml` from PET's `master` at run time, in place of `runtimes-matrix.json.uris` and a local template snapshot. PET's endpoint list is actively probed and ranked by an endpoint-health workflow; PET's template honors that ranking (`shuffle_endpoints: false`) and sets `request_timeout_seconds: 90` for heavy upstream queries that exceed Subway's default 30s per-upstream timeout. Keeping local copies of either was a recipe for drift.

- [x] Does not require a CHANGELOG entry

pr_details.title: Configurable salary parameters for Fellowship and Secretary
pr_details.author: muharem
pr_details.body:
Integrates `pallet-parameters` to the Collectives runtime and exposes the Fellowship and Secretary salary configurations as dynamic parameters that can be updated on-chain.

Supports: https://collectives.subsquare.io/fellowship/referenda/526

Dynamic parameters:
- `fellowship_salary::SalaryConfig` -`{ asset, budget }`
- `secretary_salary::SalaryConfig` -`{ asset, budget, salary_rank1 }`

`asset` is a `VersionedLocatableAsset` defaulting to USDT on Asset Hub. `budget` and `salary_rank1` are raw asset units (decimals included).

Access: Root, the FellowshipAdmin origin, or a vote among Fellows.

Notes:
- since budgets and salaries are stored as raw units, changing the asset (different decimals) requires updating those values together with it.
- for the Fellowship, the per-rank salaries stored in `pallet-core-fellowship` are also raw asset units and must be updated alongside the asset change.
- asset/value updates should be scheduled at the end of the current salary payout period to avoid mid-period mismatches between budgets and outstanding claims.

pr_details.title: Flexible minimum validator set size on RCs
pr_details.author: kianenigma
pr_details.body:
And, this PR lowers the default limits to: 

* 100 for Kusama
* 250 for Polkadot

Reasoning: 

* Context: `MinimumValidatorSetSize` on the RCs is not a parameter that controls the validator set-size in any way (that's `staking.ValidatorCount` in AHs). It is only a safety mechanism that ensures no matter what AH does, RC ignores a set smaller than this size. 
* The `StakingAsync` on AHs already has strict checks where any set smaller than `ValidatorCount` are rejected on the AH side.
* Why keeping `MinimumValidatorSetSize` flexible via a storage item vs. `const`? Because if not, any reduction of validator set requires a runtime upgrade on the RC first, slowing down the process. 
* Why `pallet-parameters` for the flexibility? Unlike `parameter_types { pub storage }` the origin can be flexible, and the proposal to upgrade it is more transparent than a `system.setStorage`.
* Why lower defaults? 
  * As mentioned above, these lower limits won't have any meaningful impact, until `Staking.ValidatorCount` on AH is changed.  
  * Since reduction of sets [is already envisioned](https://forum.polkadot.network/t/polkadot-staking-changes-progress-timeline/17436) as a part of the DAP proposal, this flexibility + lower defaults speeds up that effort. 
* Why 100 and 250? They follow the the core count and usage in each respective network.

pr_details.title: Secretary Collective: Budget Increase
pr_details.author: jessechejieh
pr_details.body: The Secretary Collective is onboarding a [Technical Coordinator](https://collectives.subsquare.io/posts/32), this change doubles the monthly budget.

pr_details.title: refactor(asset-hubs): use upstream multi-asset bounty source types
pr_details.author: dhirajs0
pr_details.body:
Closes issue #1071 

## Description

Asset Hub runtimes were using local wrapper source types from `system-parachains-common`
for `multi-asset bounties` and `child-bounties`. Those wrappers were already marked for
removal and duplicated logic that now exists upstream in `pallet_multi_asset_bounties`.

This PR removes the duplicate local module, wires both Asset Hub runtimes to the upstream
source types directly, and migrates the funds of any existing multi-asset bounty/child-bounty
to the new account derivation introduced upstream in
[paritytech/polkadot-sdk#11052](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11052).

## What Changed

### Source-type consolidation
- Switch `BountySource`/`ChildBountySource` in both runtimes to:
  - `pallet_multi_asset_bounties::BountySourceFromPalletId`
  - `pallet_multi_asset_bounties::ChildBountySourceFromPalletId`
- Use upstream prefix constants:
  - `pallet_multi_asset_bounties::BountyAccountPrefix` (`*b"mbt"`)
  - `pallet_multi_asset_bounties::ChildBountyAccountPrefix` (`*b"mcb"`)
- Remove local implementation module: `system-parachains/common/src/multi_asset_bounty_sources.rs`
- Remove the `multi-asset-bounties` optional feature from `system-parachains/common/Cargo.toml`
  and from both Asset Hub `Cargo.toml`s.
- Regenerate lockfile entries affected by the dependency cleanup.

### Encoding shift (why a migration is needed)
The wrapper's prefix was `&'static str` (`"mbt"` / `"mcb"`); the upstream prefix is `[u8; 3]`
(`*b"mbt"` / `*b"mcb"`). SCALE-encodes differently: `&str` carries a 1-byte compact-length
tag, `[u8; 3]` does not — so the sub-account seeds (and therefore the derived AccountIds) are
not byte-identical. Upstream's `[u8; 3]` is deliberate (predictable seed size, see SDK PR);
Asset-Hub-Westend already runs on this encoding. We converge KAH/PAH to it via a one-shot
balance migration.

### Migration: `MigrateBountyAccountAssets`
For each entry in `pallet_multi_asset_bounties::Bounties` and `ChildBounties`:
- Compute the OLD AccountId (legacy `&str` derivation) and the NEW AccountId (upstream
  `[u8; 3]` derivation).
- Use the runtime's existing `pallet_bounties::Config::TransferAllAssets`
  (`TransferAllFungibles<AccountId, NativeAndAssets, BountyRelevantAssets>`) to sweep every
  fungible the pot might hold from OLD → NEW.
- On KAH the migration uses a *local* `BountyMigrationAssets` parameter that extends
  `BountyRelevantAssets` with DOT, since multi-asset bounties on KAH are funded in DOT (a
  foreign asset the legacy bounties pallet does not need to handle). KAH's
  `BountyRelevantAssets` is left untouched.
- Native account collisions with the legacy `pallet-bounties` are not possible — legacy uses
  `"bt"`/`"cb"` prefixes, multi-asset uses `"mbt"`/`"mcb"`, so the derived sub-accounts are
  disjoint. Sweeping native is safe.

### Tests
Two end-to-end migration tests (one per runtime) seed a `Bounties` entry, credit the OLD
pot account with each asset class, run `on_runtime_upgrade()`, and assert the funds land at
the NEW account:
- PAH: native DOT + USDT + USDC.
- KAH: native KSM + foreign DOT + USDT.
Each test also credits an unrelated asset to confirm it is left at the OLD account.

## Scope / Behavior

Behavior of the multi-asset bounties pallet itself is unchanged. Account *derivation* shifts
to the upstream byte-for-byte form; the migration moves funds to match.

linked_issues.title: update multi-asset bounties pallet account derivation (mbt/mcb) so runtimes can drop local types

pr_details.title: Add elastic scaling zombienet tests for Polkadot AH and PC
pr_details.author: sandreim
pr_details.body:
Add some basic ES tests to ensure we produce the expected amount of blocks with 1 and 3 cores.
To ensure the test throughput is stable, we are now waiting on all validators to have compiled the PVF. Without this change, the PVF compilation would have happened during the measurement period making the test flaky. 

I've also fixed the existing smoke test.

pr_details.title: people-polkadot: Allow multiple blocks per slot + Release 2.2.2
pr_details.author: skunert
pr_details.body: We are seeing in various collator logs `Slot must increase` and only infrequent block building. With ES, we need to allow multiple blocks per slot.

pr_details.title: chain-spec-generator: remove coretime_polkadot_config and bulletin_polkadot_config
pr_details.author: bkontur
pr_details.body:
Relates to discussion: https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1146#discussion_r3082734647
Relates to: https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-bulletin-chain/issues/343


These live chain spec functions don't need to be maintained after release: the next commit after a release will use a different runtime WASM_BINARY, producing a different genesis hash, so updating bootNodes here doesn't help.


<!-- Remember that you can run `/merge` to enable auto-merge in the PR -->

Instead, if you have already modified it, simply delete the following line. -->

pr_details.title: CI skip AI review on draft and label for extended check
pr_details.author: ggwpez
pr_details.body:
- Add `ci-extended-checks` label to run extended checks at will (PET, Zombienet)
- Skip AI review on draft code (drafts are not ready for review)

[x] Does not require a CHANGELOG entry

pr_details.title: Integrate SDK 2604
pr_details.author: ggwpez
pr_details.body:
Integrates SDK release 2604.

## Contracts

- [x] @0xRVE — [#11460](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11460) `CallbackHandle` check
- [x] @0xRVE — [#11590](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11590) Asset-conversion precompile · [4759eb9e4](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/commit/4759eb9e402742fd3a4d3b303e7a755331a99e2c)
- [x] @pgherveou — [#11416](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11416) Auto-mapping (`AutoMap`) · [6dd3ddc6b](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/commit/6dd3ddc6be113f32585e6b11dee4be98dd806bdf) https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1165
- [x] @mokita-j — [#11507](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11507) Revive debug enable

## Bridges

- [x] @acatangiu — [#11694](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11694) `pallet_xcm_bridge_hub_router` `UnpaidExport` flag · [635ac29a8](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/commit/635ac29a86572729df8832d2526b6f15ddf5c2b8)
- [x] @yrong — [#11354](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11354) Snowbridge inbound nonce API · [c6fa9f236](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/commit/c6fa9f23692995a9e9640466dfe2320080ced35c)

## Async backing / collator

- [x] @sandreim — [#11778](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11778) Relay-parent offset

## Account recovery

- [x] @ggwpez — [#10482](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/10482) pallet-recovery modernization (HoldConsideration follow-up + re-bench) · [7401ea900](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/commit/7401ea90008b841f98ab037c8ba1675185efbe4b)

## Penpal / integration tests

- [x] @clangenb — [#10726](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/10726) Penpal merged `Assets`/`ForeignAssets` (downstream fixups) · [3a8aa4280](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/commit/3a8aa42800e7ed90829254e0913623b7e4a602df)
- [x] @clangenb — Encointer pallet integration 2604: Revert this afterwards c944b09a2d2991d14ff6bde77ba657fbf32af1a2

## General

- [ ] Re-benchmark weights #1163 
- [x] Check for in-code TODOs
- [x] Check for all migrations
